heroHeadline: Arakan Army Storms Sittwe Naval Base as Junta Stages 'Civilian' Presidential Transfer
heroSubtitle: On March 23, the Arakan Army launched a major assault on the Shwe Min Gan Naval Base outside Sittwe — the last significant junta holdout in Rakhine State — killing an estimated 30–40 SAC troops and advancing fighters to within 2 km of the city center. Simultaneously, the junta's military-packed parliament convened on March 16 and Armed Forces Day (March 27) saw Vice-Senior General Soe Win announce an imminent presidential selection, with Min Aung Hlaing expected to assume the role. The AAPP death toll reached 7,941+ killed as of March 23. ACLED has documented 5,912 airstrikes and 931 drone attacks since the 2021 coup, with resistance forces targeting Magway Air Base with 11 rockets on March 25.
footerNote: Data sourced from AAPP, ACLED, UN OCHA, Irrawaddy, RFA, Myanmar Now, DVB, Al Jazeera, Mizzima, Human Rights Watch, Crisis Group, Myanmar Peace Monitor, and official NUG, KNU, and AA statements.
